speaker: QUEEN
text: What sport shall we devise here in this garden

speaker: QUEEN
text: To drive away the heavy thought of care?

speaker: LADY
text: Madam, we’ll play at bowls.

speaker: QUEEN
text: ’Twill make me think the world is full of rubs

speaker: QUEEN
text: And that my fortune runs against the bias.

speaker: LADY
text: Madam, we’ll dance.

speaker: QUEEN
text: My legs can keep no measure in delight

speaker: QUEEN
text: When my poor heart no measure keeps in grief.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Therefore no dancing, girl. Some other sport.

speaker: LADY
text: Madam, we’ll tell tales.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Of sorrow or of joy?

speaker: LADY
text: Of either, madam.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Of neither, girl,

speaker: QUEEN
text: For if of joy, being altogether wanting,

speaker: QUEEN
text: It doth remember me the more of sorrow;

speaker: QUEEN
text: Or if of grief, being altogether had,

speaker: QUEEN
text: It adds more sorrow to my want of joy.

speaker: QUEEN
text: For what I have I need not to repeat,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And what I want it boots not to complain.

speaker: LADY
text: Madam, I’ll sing.

speaker: QUEEN
text: ’Tis well that thou hast cause,

speaker: QUEEN
text: But thou shouldst please me better wouldst thou weep.

speaker: LADY
text: I could weep, madam, would it do you good.

speaker: QUEEN
text: And I could sing, would weeping do me good,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And never borrow any tear of thee.

speaker: QUEEN
text: But stay, here come the gardeners.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Let’s step into the shadow of these trees.

speaker: QUEEN
text: My wretchedness unto a row of pins,

speaker: QUEEN
text: They will talk of state, for everyone doth so

speaker: QUEEN
text: Against a change. Woe is forerun with woe.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Go, bind thou up young dangling apricokes

speaker: GARDENER
text: Which, like unruly children, make their sire

speaker: GARDENER
text: Stoop with oppression of their prodigal weight.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Give some supportance to the bending twigs.—

speaker: GARDENER
text: Go thou, and like an executioner

speaker: GARDENER
text: Cut off the heads of too-fast-growing sprays

speaker: GARDENER
text: That look too lofty in our commonwealth.

speaker: GARDENER
text: All must be even in our government.

speaker: GARDENER
text: You thus employed, I will go root away

speaker: GARDENER
text: The noisome weeds which without profit suck

speaker: GARDENER
text: The soil’s fertility from wholesome flowers.

speaker: MAN
text: Why should we, in the compass of a pale,

speaker: MAN
text: Keep law and form and due proportion,

speaker: MAN
text: Showing as in a model our firm estate,

speaker: MAN
text: When our sea-wallèd garden, the whole land,

speaker: MAN
text: Is full of weeds, her fairest flowers choked up,

speaker: MAN
text: Her fruit trees all unpruned, her hedges ruined,

speaker: MAN
text: Her knots disordered, and her wholesome herbs

speaker: MAN
text: Swarming with caterpillars?

speaker: GARDENER
text: Hold thy peace.

speaker: GARDENER
text: He that hath suffered this disordered spring

speaker: GARDENER
text: Hath now himself met with the fall of leaf.

speaker: GARDENER
text: The weeds which his broad-spreading leaves did shelter,

speaker: GARDENER
text: That seemed in eating him to hold him up,

speaker: GARDENER
text: Are plucked up, root and all, by Bolingbroke—

speaker: GARDENER
text: I mean the Earl of Wiltshire, Bushy, Green.

speaker: MAN
text: What, are they dead?

speaker: GARDENER
text: They are. And Bolingbroke

speaker: GARDENER
text: Hath seized the wasteful king. O, what pity is it

speaker: GARDENER
text: That he had not so trimmed and dressed his land

speaker: GARDENER
text: As we this garden! We at time of year

speaker: GARDENER
text: Do wound the bark, the skin of our fruit trees,

speaker: GARDENER
text: Lest, being overproud in sap and blood,

speaker: GARDENER
text: With too much riches it confound itself.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Had he done so to great and growing men,

speaker: GARDENER
text: They might have lived to bear and he to taste

speaker: GARDENER
text: Their fruits of duty. Superfluous branches

speaker: GARDENER
text: We lop away, that bearing boughs may live.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Had he done so, himself had borne the crown,

speaker: GARDENER
text: Which waste of idle hours hath quite thrown down.

speaker: MAN
text: What, think you the King shall be deposed?

speaker: GARDENER
text: Depressed he is already, and deposed

speaker: GARDENER
text: ’Tis doubt he will be. Letters came last night

speaker: GARDENER
text: To a dear friend of the good Duke of York’s

speaker: GARDENER
text: That tell black tidings.

speaker: QUEEN
text: O, I am pressed to death through want of speaking!

speaker: QUEEN
text: Thou old Adam’s likeness, set to dress this garden,

speaker: QUEEN
text: How dares thy harsh rude tongue sound this unpleasing news?

speaker: QUEEN
text: What Eve, what serpent, hath suggested thee

speaker: QUEEN
text: To make a second fall of cursèd man?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Why dost thou say King Richard is deposed?

speaker: QUEEN
text: Dar’st thou, thou little better thing than earth,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Divine his downfall? Say where, when, and how

speaker: QUEEN
text: Cam’st thou by this ill tidings? Speak, thou wretch!

speaker: GARDENER
text: Pardon me, madam. Little joy have I

speaker: GARDENER
text: To breathe this news, yet what I say is true.

speaker: GARDENER
text: King Richard, he is in the mighty hold

speaker: GARDENER
text: Of Bolingbroke. Their fortunes both are weighed.

speaker: GARDENER
text: In your lord’s scale is nothing but himself

speaker: GARDENER
text: And some few vanities that make him light,

speaker: GARDENER
text: But in the balance of great Bolingbroke,

speaker: GARDENER
text: Besides himself, are all the English peers,

speaker: GARDENER
text: And with that odds he weighs King Richard down.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Post you to London and you will find it so.

speaker: GARDENER
text: I speak no more than everyone doth know.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Nimble mischance, that art so light of foot,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Doth not thy embassage belong to me,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And am I last that knows it? O, thou thinkest

speaker: QUEEN
text: To serve me last that I may longest keep

speaker: QUEEN
text: Thy sorrow in my breast. Come, ladies, go

speaker: QUEEN
text: To meet at London London’s king in woe.

speaker: QUEEN
text: What, was I born to this, that my sad look

speaker: QUEEN
text: Should grace the triumph of great Bolingbroke?—

speaker: QUEEN
text: Gard’ner, for telling me these news of woe,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Pray God the plants thou graft’st may never grow.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Poor queen, so that thy state might be no worse,

speaker: GARDENER
text: I would my skill were subject to thy curse.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Here did she fall a tear. Here in this place

speaker: GARDENER
text: I’ll set a bank of rue, sour herb of grace.

speaker: GARDENER
text: Rue even for ruth here shortly shall be seen

speaker: GARDENER
text: In the remembrance of a weeping queen.
